transcript.text: Ashley, Veronica, how have you been?
Hey, Tyler.
Good.
How are you?
Yeah, I'm doing great.
Doing great.
Wrapping up some wedding planning with my fiance, and we're ready for our planner.
We didn't do the full planner.
We did the month of, and it's in April, but we're ready for her to take over.
Where are you guys doing it?
Uh, we are both born and raised in Houston, so we're going to keep it local, make it easy on our friends and family.
Yeah.
Nice.
But I'm excited.
The planner is a very, very good call, especially, like, once you get to day of, like, not having to think of anything, like, on your actual wedding day is, like, magical.
So, you'll be glad.
I couldn't even imagine, and we're doing a ceremony venue that's separate from the reception.
So, like, ceremonies at the Houston Botanic Gardens, and then receptions.
In this cool industrial space that's in like a popular neighborhood near downtown.
So like even the logistics of getting people from point A to point B alone would be too much to deal with.
Yeah.
Yeah.
And there's like things I heard of like after my wedding that like things that happened.
And I was like, I didn't even know that happened.
And I love that for myself.
Like, you know, yeah.
Everybody.
Hey, I'm Brian.
By the way, Tyler, congrats in advance.
One thing I tell everyone that's having an upcoming wedding, because someone told me and I'm so glad they did.
During the ceremony, you'll be nervous and distracted and a lot of things will be going down your head.
Stop, look back at everyone.
And it's the coolest thing ever because like you never see that many people that are like that genuinely happy.
It's just like a really amazing image that's like seared into my mind.
And so I'd recommend doing the same thing.
Oh, I love that, Brian.
And yeah, thank you.
And I will take your advice.
But.

I'll be cautious because I can be a bit emotional and I'm trying to keep the tears from flowing too strong in front of all my friends and family because they're definitely going to come out and that'll be a big trigger right there.
I'm already like thinking about it and getting a little emotional.
The good news is that I'll just make everyone smile more.
So I'll just make it all the better.
But anyway, great, great, great to meet.
Yeah, yeah.
Great to meet you.
I've heard a lot of awesome things from the partnership we've had with Sentinel One and I've enjoyed getting to meet the team and then dived deeper with Lane earlier this week.
My plan for today was I was going to kind of just start off by going through some analysis that we did and kind of like talking about the partnership high level.

And then I figured I would just like open it up to the team to dig in with any specific questions and obviously like we can get back under the hood and like deconstruct how the content gets created, how the strategy gets set.
But, um.

You know, can you find a partner who can really lead when it comes to strategy and not just lead when it comes to strategy, but take you to a much higher scale so that you can start to see signals and results much faster?
You know, I know that that's one piece is, you know, you're not really creating content as at as fast of a rate as you would like.
And that we hear that all the time, right?
Like teams aren't getting any bigger.
It's it's difficult to be able to create content at scale when, you know, you haven't stitched together these workflows where AI can come into the picture at different parts of the process.
And then I think, Lane, you really helped me get a good sense of this, that the focus is not entirely or the focus is not really on the MSP.
It's on the enterprise opportunity.
And so really making sure that as you're restructuring the site, you know, we think about the content that is going to lead to the enterprise leads in particular.
It sounds good.
And those are some of the main challenges you've experienced.
Yeah.
Cool.
And then in terms of the competitive landscape, I know Citrix is a main competitor.
I know we haven't talked too much about the other three here.
Some of the opportunities that we see really is non-branded traffic.
The other companies have a larger percentage of non-branded compared to the amount that you have today.
So just being able to target some of the highest intent keywords within-branded, being able to grow in terms of the long tail, the amount of keywords that you're ranking for.
Citrix, I know they do a lot of different things, but I was pretty amazed with how many keywords that they rank for.
And we did see that there's a lot of comparison content that we could create that for our other clients has led to quick results.
lot of with, um, um, um,
LLMs and we have a really nice way of like doing this in more of an objective lens and not, you know, talking down on the competitors, but helping buyers educate themselves more in the market.

And then laying to like your earlier comments, liquidware and control up, sounds like they're more on like the MSP side, but we do see an opportunity to really dominate some of the traffic that they're getting that's complimentary to yours today.
And we also see that the competitors haven't really focused on AEO too much in terms of like what we're seeing in answers today, what we're seeing in citations.

And so I think, you know, like with the Microsoft partnership, with the competitors moving a bit slower on the AEO side, some opportunities that we could tackle would be, you know, one, we tend to refresh clients' content every three to six months.
And so I think there's like probably a.
One of the main values.
I think there's additional pillars that we will be coming up with.
Like, it's definitely one of, like, the primary value prop pillars, but I think there'll be others that we'll develop and start to lean into.
And so I think there's probably other unknown opportunities right there, too, of, like, what are the other kind of authority hubs that we would want to be known for.

I think another thing that I was thinking about in, like, the section above is I don't know that we've, like, sorry, like, the section you were at before this when you were talking about, like, competitors and things like that.
I think there's an opportunity for, like, how do we run along, like, and what they're talking about and be, like, because I think people are going to think about Microsoft first.
And then, like, then we need to say, like, this is where Nerdio plays with Microsoft.
So I think there's a portion of that of Microsoft is our strongest.
Partner, so it's not necessarily a competitor, but like we need to be thought of alongside them just as much as we are like some of like the competitors that we're going after.
I love that.
Yeah.
Alongside Microsoft versus just focusing on the competitors.
Is that one differentiator that competitors don't have, the relationship you have with Microsoft or how your solution is?
We call them competitors.
All like, to be honest, they are Microsoft competitors and we are benefiting from them moving.
So like Nerdio is not necessarily a direct competitor to Citrix.
What is the competitive motion there is moving people off of like legacy VDI systems like Citrix onto Microsoft.
We help that migration piece go easier.
Microsoft also works with Citrix, so they are not going to go out and have like a big competitive motion rounds.
out big,
Citrix, but I do want people off of legacy VDI platforms onto their cloud platform so we can help with that motion.
So that's where it's like weird.
Like if NerdioCon doesn't have, or not NerdioCon, that's our event I'm thinking about.
Nerdio does not have like necessarily big direct competitors where you're out.
We're just trying to make noise and market around Microsoft's competitors that we can help move people off of them onto Microsoft products more easily.
Does that make sense?
It makes sense.

And one of the reasons I don't go super deep in the sales process here is because on the kickoff call, our team asks like a bunch of discovery questions that are brought by design to capture the nuance of like how you position your product against competitors, who you think of as competitors, what like your three best customers would say about what makes Nerdio, right?
The best option.
And so this is definitely helpful for me to just hand off to the audience.
The team that you'd be working with in phase one if we moved forward.
The other piece.
Oh, yeah, go ahead.
One other thing that I think we just need to be thoughtful about is Microsoft is pushing really hard on Windows 365.
It's sort of their hero skew because they're going to make way more money than they will on anything sort of Azure virtual desktop related.
And I was going through just like a basic Google Trends search and W365 searches are like pretty in line with overall virtual desktop.
They're a little bit below, but they're above Azure virtual desktop searches.

And so I don't know how much content we're already creating, but knowing that's where Microsoft's trying to push people and there's a lot of search volume, I feel like we should be trying to capture that space.
That's super helpful, Brian.
And a lot of search volume around Windows 365.
Correct.
Yeah.
It's sort of like that.
Think of it as like the next iteration as people are trying to virtualize their desktop and take advantage of Microsoft's capabilities.
Got it.
How's the searches for Windows Vista?
I'm just kidding.
Cool.
And there's some other pieces here too.

We talk about like the shift from on-prem VDI to cloud desktops and the journey, being able to have content that meets an IT leader at each stage, whether they're evaluating, migrating, deploying, optimizing, expanding, really just like filling in anything that is a void in terms of like Microsoft documentation or like competitor content around the stages, right, in that life cycle.
And obviously with the AI search side, we would set up a bunch of the most relevant prompts for you in our internal platform.
And you'd be able to leverage that as part of the He's was of
The relationship is all the prompts that are relevant and tracked against, you know, the top companies in your space that you're focused on seeing progress against.
And so, you know, we would handle on the services side, all the strategy, all the content creation.
Your team would be partners in reviewing the artifacts that we create that are the context engineering for our workflows, the drafts for the articles that we're producing.
Again, we'll look at five a week, and this will be a combination of net new and refresh.

And then all that visibility and monitoring, we'll handle setting up that, and you'll have the ability to log into that platform and add prompts or, you know, do bespoke analysis or even, you know, make changes.
So, in short, I think the theme is we definitely love to lead as a partner in terms of strategy and in terms of execution.
But our best.

partnerships come from collaboration with points of view, like you've given me today around, let's experiment with optimizing, let's experiment with prioritizing Microsoft 365 types of searches, or these topic clusters are great, but this one's more catered to MSPs, and we're focused on more of like the enterprise buyer.
That's the sort of feedback that we need to execute in the right lanes.

All right, and just to call out, because like I'm newer to the space and I'm getting up to speed, Windows 365 is a different thing than Microsoft 365, it's Windows 365, that's the one that's more relevant for our space.
For MSP stuff, Microsoft 365 is also, but Windows 365 is what we prioritize.
lot of similarities.
confusing, it's fine.
Wow, they're two different things?
Yep.
That makes sense.
That makes sense.
I guess Windows is a part of Microsoft, but not is Microsoft.
But is it Microsoft entirely?
Cool.
Well, I hope some of this analysis is helpful.
I'm happy to just open it up to the team and want to be an open book and dig as deep as necessary to help ensure that you feel confident in our approach and ability to make an impact.
Tyler, a question for you.
don't know if you've covered this with the team, but it was something that we did at SentinelOne, and I don't know if you still offer it.
But we had signed up for a year contract with the ability to sort of exit sort of at the three-month mark if we weren't seeing value.
Is that something you still offer?

Today, how we set it up is, I'm not sure if there was the strategy sprint when we started with SentinelOne, but what we do is we give you until the last week of the strategy sprint to opt out so that you can see the quality we can hit with our...
Our articles and the roadmap that we would execute around, but based on the way we're measured, we're not doing any more termination for convenience.
It would just be breach.

We're going to give the team the ability to use our platform that we execute on internally, and all of the VCE conversations we have, it's measuring the business on software metrics, and so that's why we don't have an opt-out in the 12-month term of the partnership.
When does that strategy period end?
It's eight weeks, and so you'd have until the last week to make it all Okay, it's two months instead of three months.
Essentially, yes.
Yeah.
Yeah.
That would have been an easier way to explain.
Yes.
50 days.
49 days.
Yeah.
Okay, cool.
And then you might have gotten into this with like, what is the pricing structure?
I don't think I've had that discussion, or if I've asked that question.
It hasn't stuck.
I'm sorry.
Oh, yeah, no problem.
I don't think we've had that discussion, to be honest with you.
So let me be transparent there.
We do $40,000 for the strategy sprint, and then it's $18,000 per month on the growth execution.
Definitely can be something that we have a conversation around.
We just ask that as we have that conversation, we get red lines back on the agreement so that we can have that conversation together, right?
What terms do we need to adjust and where do we need to compromise or meet in the middle on price so that you have the budget to move forward?
And just on that front, because one of the things we did the last time around was we were determining sort of how many sort of different things we were doing.
And so in that case, there were sort of like three different use cases that we were solving for, which played into the price.
Like what's the determinant for if it's $18,000 or more or less?
Is it sort of volume of content, number of like different areas that you're creating content around?
How are you thinking about that?
Yeah, we look at it as work streams.
And so this would be a work stream that would get you five editorial articles per week.
What we like to do is, you know, let's say we've done a lot of editorial and we want to move to programmatic.
And then we have had some time to kind of think through what are the inputs to make that programmatic use case or to create that program, to execute that programmatic use case at scale.
What we could do is we could always switch from editorial to programmatic.

But if you wanted us to do like both at the same time or maybe a completely custom use case, like one of the work streams for Sentinel one was probably the vulnerability database, but I would need to look at the contract to know for sure.
Maybe they were going to be like, you know, custom design resources from our side that are executing on that or even developers.
A work stream would be anywhere from 12 to 18K.
Thank you.
you.

Without the prospect of being able to get better signal, like I said, I think during our first call, Tyler, about what's working and what's not as far as topics and places where we could dig in deeper.
So I feel good.
That's awesome.
And the thing I'll point out, about this timeline is that we can definitely do more than five articles.
We strive to do over 10, in some cases closer to 20.
So it really just comes down to like how fast we calibrate.
And we're not going to day one, you know, write like your subject matter experts would.
But what we are.
Yeah, because it's an interesting space.
Like there are deep experts in the space.
And it's not something, you know, that I think general AI has successfully gotten yet.
So I have full confidence that we can.
I'm just saying.
I'm just pointing it out that I think that's a really good call out, Tyler.
Absolutely.
No, I appreciate that.
And what helps us a lot is like having the right resources.
So if there are some really good product marketing, one pagers.

Or even, you know, pieces of content that you think represents great, either from you or other companies that represent good examples that we can sort of train our workflows around, then that makes a massive impact.
That's the good news, because we have a ton of good content that our SMEs have worked on.
So I think it makes training that much easier.
So that's awesome.
And then I think, too, in terms of, you know, you mentioned, like, you're getting six glossary articles, four referral articles that Microsoft publishes or uses each month.
Lane, I think we can absolutely, like, create both of those types very easily as part of the standard scope, sort of, like, customizations, if there's a need.
Or we could, like, execute alongside that partner.
But the content that we create, like, it could be leveraged for the partner as well as content that we stage and publish in your CM.
And so this is like another element where we have flexibility.
Just let us know, like, I guess, what would be more beneficial or what would be more of a priority as something that we would come in and take on, right?
Versus like the person that you're working with today.
Great.
Cool.
Well, happy to spend more time if anyone needs it.
But otherwise, I will send the contract out for your review, Brian.
Feel free to redline it up, whatever makes you feel comfortable and confident.
And then we'll have a conversation with Marcel to see where we can land.
And right now we're booking for the first week in March with our kickoffs.
So we would prioritize, assuming we can get this done in, you know, a week or a little over a week, making sure you have a kickoff, you know, by mid-March at the absolute latest.
Great.
And then, then,

Just a question, team on our side, because when we were, when I was at the center, when we were ramping with growthx, the two biggest sort of bottlenecks that we had were one, our product marketing team wanted to review everything in detail, and they were just taking a really long time to review it.
And then the second thing was, we were then posting all of the content or wanting to post it to begin with, rather than giving growthx access.
I don't know if we have a point of view on sort of both of those fronts in terms of how we've been doing things today.
But that, that ultimately was, growthx was creating content faster than we could do things with content.
On the posting front, that we're in the process of setting up permission, so it'll be easy to give them access to post things.
Product marketing, I'm not sure I can see that being a little bit of a bottleneck.
Yeah, of course it's just been very close.

They aligned to the current partner we are working with, Brian, and so by, so one, I think we would need to see if she has, like, time to go along in the journey and, like, the strategy process and the setup to give her confidence in, like, what we are doing.
I, my hope would be maybe, like, at first we're having them review, but if we can get some traction and confidence, then maybe that review process can be skipped a little bit eventually.
I don't know your thoughts there.

Tyler, this is maybe an outdated comment, so feel free to tell me if it, if it is, but my recollection was for the content that you're creating for each section of the content, you're essentially giving it, like, a QA score of, of some kind, and, and, and as part of that, I think what I'd love to be able to do is align with Carissa and team on, hey, you know, we believe if it's, I forget the numbers, but let's call it a five, we have.
I have very high confidence that that is fine, but if it's a two, then that's something that we definitely want to review.
But something like that might be a good way to over time narrow sort of the level of review required for different parts.
But Tyler, correct me if things have maybe changed since the last time I was thinking about that.
No, that makes sense.
That's definitely how we're looking at it.
Every article that hits your desk has been reviewed by our team internally.
We have different evals that are done through the content creation process with the workflows themselves.
So it's really up to you like how much you want to review.
But we typically have two, you know, at most three rounds of review at the beginning.
And then we move to an operating cadence where after you've seen us publish probably 10 articles, it starts to turn into just one review.
And every time we get feedback, it helps us optimize the artifacts.
So standard to have some more review up front and less later on.
But we don't really have like a score.
You can have it ready to review when you've had a chance to align with the team.
And if you need another conversation, Brian, to feel confident either way, just let me know.
I thought Marcel was going to be able to join this one, but if you tell me he needs to be there, then I'll make sure he's there.
Sounds good.
Veronica, think you probably need to run to an internal team meeting.
I don't if you do, but I was going to see if we could at least just grab five minutes.
I'll send you both, send this review link and Veronica, you can join on that.
That'd be great.
Okay.
Bye, everybody.
Nice.
Bye.
See y'all.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Deep dive on a potential content partnership between GrowthX and Nerdio.](https://fathom.video/share/nMWdmVARz78xTxyDePR-ekmKGXF4xxkH?tab=summary&timestamp=205.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**Strategic Focus:** Shift content from MSPs to enterprise buyers, focusing on moving them from legacy VDI (e.g., Citrix) to Microsoft cloud platforms.](https://fathom.video/share/nMWdmVARz78xTxyDePR-ekmKGXF4xxkH?tab=summary&timestamp=338.0)
  - [**Content Priority:** Capture high search volume for Windows 365 (W365), Microsoft's hero SKU, which has search volume comparable to "virtual desktop" and exceeds "Azure virtual desktop."](https://fathom.video/share/nMWdmVARz78xTxyDePR-ekmKGXF4xxkH?tab=summary&timestamp=789.0)
  - [**Partnership Terms:** $40k strategy sprint, then $18k/mo for 5+ articles/week. A 7-week opt-out period is offered, but no "termination for convenience" clause exists in the 12-month term.](https://fathom.video/share/nMWdmVARz78xTxyDePR-ekmKGXF4xxkH?tab=summary&timestamp=1129.0)
  - [**Operational Risk:** Nerdio's product marketing review process is a known bottleneck. GrowthX's process aims to mitigate this by reducing review rounds from 2-3 to 1 after \~10 articles.](https://fathom.video/share/nMWdmVARz78xTxyDePR-ekmKGXF4xxkH?tab=summary&timestamp=1560.0)

## Topics

### Nerdio's Content Challenges & Strategic Shift

  - [**Primary Challenge:** Inability to produce content at scale due to limited team resources.](https://fathom.video/share/nMWdmVARz78xTxyDePR-ekmKGXF4xxkH?tab=summary&timestamp=313.0)
  - [**Strategic Pivot:** Shift focus from MSPs to enterprise buyers.](https://fathom.video/share/nMWdmVARz78xTxyDePR-ekmKGXF4xxkH?tab=summary&timestamp=338.0)
      - [**Rationale:** The new website structure is being built to attract enterprise leads.](https://fathom.video/share/nMWdmVARz78xTxyDePR-ekmKGXF4xxkH?tab=summary&timestamp=338.0)
  - [**Competitive Positioning:**](https://fathom.video/share/nMWdmVARz78xTxyDePR-ekmKGXF4xxkH?tab=summary&timestamp=428.0)
      - [Nerdio is not a direct competitor to Citrix.](https://fathom.video/share/nMWdmVARz78xTxyDePR-ekmKGXF4xxkH?tab=summary&timestamp=679.0)
      - [**Goal:** Facilitate migration *from* legacy VDI (Citrix) *to* Microsoft cloud platforms.](https://fathom.video/share/nMWdmVARz78xTxyDePR-ekmKGXF4xxkH?tab=summary&timestamp=698.0)
      - [**Strategy:** Position Nerdio *alongside* Microsoft, not just against competitors.](https://fathom.video/share/nMWdmVARz78xTxyDePR-ekmKGXF4xxkH?tab=summary&timestamp=627.0)

### Content Strategy & Opportunities

  - [**Key Opportunity:** Capture non-branded traffic by targeting high-intent keywords and building long-tail keyword authority.](https://fathom.video/share/nMWdmVARz78xTxyDePR-ekmKGXF4xxkH?tab=summary&timestamp=437.0)
  - [**Content Pillars:**](https://fathom.video/share/nMWdmVARz78xTxyDePR-ekmKGXF4xxkH?tab=summary&timestamp=462.0)
      - [**Comparison Content:** Create objective articles (e.g., Nerdio vs. Citrix) to educate buyers and drive quick results.](https://fathom.video/share/nMWdmVARz78xTxyDePR-ekmKGXF4xxkH?tab=summary&timestamp=462.0)
      - [**Windows 365 (W365):** Prioritize content for this high-volume term, which is Microsoft's hero SKU and has search volume comparable to "virtual desktop."](https://fathom.video/share/nMWdmVARz78xTxyDePR-ekmKGXF4xxkH?tab=summary&timestamp=789.0)
      - [**AEO (Answer Engine Optimization):** Dominate this area, as competitors are moving slowly.](https://fathom.video/share/nMWdmVARz78xTxyDePR-ekmKGXF4xxkH?tab=summary&timestamp=508.0)
  - [**Content Lifecycle:** Create content for each stage of the cloud desktop journey: evaluation, migration, deployment, optimization, and expansion.](https://fathom.video/share/nMWdmVARz78xTxyDePR-ekmKGXF4xxkH?tab=summary&timestamp=860.0)

### GrowthX Partnership Model & Terms

  - [**Services:** GrowthX provides strategy, content creation, and performance monitoring.](https://fathom.video/share/nMWdmVARz78xTxyDePR-ekmKGXF4xxkH?tab=summary&timestamp=913.0)
  - [**Deliverables:**](https://fathom.video/share/nMWdmVARz78xTxyDePR-ekmKGXF4xxkH?tab=summary&timestamp=913.0)
      - [**Volume:** 5 editorial articles per week, with potential to scale to 10-20 as workflows calibrate.](https://fathom.video/share/nMWdmVARz78xTxyDePR-ekmKGXF4xxkH?tab=summary&timestamp=1394.0)
      - [**Content Types:** Includes glossary and referral articles for Microsoft.](https://fathom.video/share/nMWdmVARz78xTxyDePR-ekmKGXF4xxkH?tab=summary&timestamp=1465.0)
  - [**Pricing:**](https://fathom.video/share/nMWdmVARz78xTxyDePR-ekmKGXF4xxkH?tab=summary&timestamp=1129.0)
      - [**Strategy Sprint:** $40,000 (8 weeks).](https://fathom.video/share/nMWdmVARz78xTxyDePR-ekmKGXF4xxkH?tab=summary&timestamp=1129.0)
      - [**Growth Execution:** $18,000 per month (12-month term).](https://fathom.video/share/nMWdmVARz78xTxyDePR-ekmKGXF4xxkH?tab=summary&timestamp=1129.0)
  - [**Contract Terms:**](https://fathom.video/share/nMWdmVARz78xTxyDePR-ekmKGXF4xxkH?tab=summary&timestamp=1050.0)
      - [**Opt-Out Period:** 7 weeks (until the final week of the strategy sprint).](https://fathom.video/share/nMWdmVARz78xTxyDePR-ekmKGXF4xxkH?tab=summary&timestamp=1050.0)
      - [**Termination:** No "for convenience" clause; termination requires a breach of contract.](https://fathom.video/share/nMWdmVARz78xTxyDePR-ekmKGXF4xxkH?tab=summary&timestamp=1050.0)

### Operational Bottlenecks & Mitigation

  - [**Nerdio's Concerns (from SentinelOne experience):**](https://fathom.video/share/nMWdmVARz78xTxyDePR-ekmKGXF4xxkH?tab=summary&timestamp=1560.0)
    1.  [**Product Marketing Reviews:** A known bottleneck due to slow turnaround.](https://fathom.video/share/nMWdmVARz78xTxyDePR-ekmKGXF4xxkH?tab=summary&timestamp=1560.0)
    2.  [**Posting Process:** Nerdio currently posts all content, which slows the publishing cadence.](https://fathom.video/share/nMWdmVARz78xTxyDePR-ekmKGXF4xxkH?tab=summary&timestamp=1560.0)
  - [**GrowthX's Solutions:**](https://fathom.video/share/nMWdmVARz78xTxyDePR-ekmKGXF4xxkH?tab=summary&timestamp=1560.0)
      - [**Review Process:** Reduce review rounds from 2-3 to 1 after \~10 articles as workflows optimize.](https://fathom.video/share/nMWdmVARz78xTxyDePR-ekmKGXF4xxkH?tab=summary&timestamp=1656.0)
      - [**Posting Access:** Nerdio is setting up permissions to grant GrowthX direct CMS access.](https://fathom.video/share/nMWdmVARz78xTxyDePR-ekmKGXF4xxkH?tab=summary&timestamp=1560.0)
      - [**Content Training:** Use Nerdio's existing high-quality SME content to accelerate workflow calibration.](https://fathom.video/share/nMWdmVARz78xTxyDePR-ekmKGXF4xxkH?tab=summary&timestamp=1431.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**Tyler:** Send contract to Brian for review and redlining.](https://fathom.video/share/nMWdmVARz78xTxyDePR-ekmKGXF4xxkH?tab=summary&timestamp=1520.0)
  - [**Brian:** Review contract with Marcel and the team, redline, and discuss pricing.](https://fathom.video/share/nMWdmVARz78xTxyDePR-ekmKGXF4xxkH?tab=summary&timestamp=1524.0)
  - [**Nerdio Team:** Align internally on the product marketing review process and posting workflow.](https://fathom.video/share/nMWdmVARz78xTxyDePR-ekmKGXF4xxkH?tab=summary&timestamp=1560.0)
  - [**Target Kickoff:** Mid-March 2026.](https://fathom.video/share/nMWdmVARz78xTxyDePR-ekmKGXF4xxkH?tab=summary&timestamp=1524.0)

action_items.description: Email Brian contract for redlines; then schedule pricing/terms call w/ Marcel
Align w/ Carissa on review workflow; then share w/ Tyler
Email Veronica review link; then hold 5-min review
